pattern: A single linear scan is enough: track the largest value and second largest, then check the dominance condition between just those two.
hints: You only ever need to compare the biggest number against the next biggest, not against every element.
Find the maximum and its index, plus the largest value among all the other elements.
If the maximum is at least twice that runner-up value, return the maximum's index, otherwise return -1.

edge_inputs.description: largest is less than twice the runner-up
exactly twice the runner-up still qualifies

stress.description: random arrays with a guaranteed unique maximum
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 50)
    arr = [rng.randint(0, 99) for _ in range(n)]
    # force a unique maximum so the premise holds
    i = rng.randrange(n)
    arr[i] = 100
    return [arr]
stress.note: constructs a unique max to satisfy the problem's guarantee
